findings.description: LazyMotion strict + motion.* = runtime crash. All 9 component files import `motion` from 'motion/react' and use motion.div/motion.aside/motion.tr etc. With `<LazyMotion features={domAnimation} strict>` in providers.tsx, EVERY motion.* element will throw a runtime error. Must use `m.*` from 'motion/react-m' instead, or remove `strict` prop.
findings.fix: Option A (recommended): Change all `import { motion } from 'motion/react'` to `import * as m from 'motion/react-m'` and replace motion.div → m.div, motion.aside → m.aside, etc. in all 9 files. Option B: Remove `strict` from LazyMotion (loses bundle-size enforcement).

findings.description: AnimatedStatCount uses imperative animate() which bypasses MotionConfig reducedMotion='user'. Users with prefers-reduced-motion will still see count-up animations.
findings.fix: Add `const prefersReduced = useReducedMotion()` from motion/react. If true, skip animate() and render the final value immediately.

findings.description: DataTable AnimatePresence uses mode='popLayout' but wraps div>table structure. popLayout requires layout animations and can cause visual glitches with table elements. The spec calls for mode='wait' on tab content switches.
findings.fix: Change `<AnimatePresence mode="popLayout">` to `<AnimatePresence mode="wait">` to match spec P3-009 and avoid layout conflicts with table elements.

findings.description: Sidebar uses two layoutId animations ('sidebar-active-bg' + 'sidebar-active-border') with different spring configs (stiffness:400, damping:35) than the spec's spring(300,30). While the snappier config works for small elements, it deviates from animation-constants.ts EASING.spring spec.
findings.fix: Use EASING.snappy from animation-constants.ts for consistency: `transition={EASING.snappy}` (which is stiffness:500, damping:40), or document the rationale for the custom values.

findings.description: DataTable rowVariants defines inline easing [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1] instead of using EASING.default from animation-constants.ts. Duration 0.2 also duplicates DURATION.normal.
findings.fix: Import EASING and DURATION from '@/lib/animation-constants' and use them: `transition: { duration: DURATION.normal, ease: EASING.default }`

findings.description: AnimatedStatCount ref merge pattern is fragile — runs on every render without deps, uses MutableRefObject cast, and doesn't handle ref cleanup.
findings.fix: Use a callback ref or `useMergeRefs` pattern from a utility library.

findings.description: No E2E test coverage for Phase 3 animations. m-p3-features.spec.ts covers M-P3 (Metrics), not P3 (animations). New components AnimatedStatCount, MobileDrawer, MotionButton have zero test coverage.
findings.fix: Add basic E2E smoke tests for sidebar collapse animation, tab transitions, and drawer open/close. At minimum, verify sidebar data-testid='sidebar' changes width on collapse toggle click.

findings.description: MotionButton component is exported but only used in DataTable PaginationBtn inline. The reusable component is never imported elsewhere.
findings.fix: Either use MotionButton in PaginationBtn and other buttons, or remove the standalone component and keep the inline pattern.

findings.description: MobileDrawer is created but never imported/used anywhere in the changed files. It's a dead export until wired up.
findings.fix: Wire MobileDrawer into pod detail or alert detail views, or mark as TODO with a comment.

findings.description: Sidebar label AnimatePresence transition uses hardcoded duration:0.15 and custom easing instead of DURATION.fast and EASING.default from animation-constants.ts.
findings.fix: Use DURATION.fast (which equals 0.15) from constants for consistency.

findings.description: BOARD.md marks items as 'v0.3.1 2025-01' but date should be 2026-03 and version should match v194.
findings.fix: Update dates and version tags to match actual release.

findings.description: logs/page.tsx panel layout has awkward JSX nesting — inner div is opened after Panel but closed before panel resize handle, making the structure hard to follow.
findings.fix: Consider extracting the upper panel content into a separate component for readability.
